title: How to verify v0.6.0 release usability (#255)
purpose: Describe exactly what the user must do to close #255 without storing private participant material in the repository.

participantEvidenceRequiredToPass.minimumStudy: Run the final moderated release-usability task study described in docs/research/v0.6-release-usability-study.json.
gates: At least 90% unassisted completion for required tasks.
Zero unrecovered critical task failures among tested participants.
Median first-transcript interactions reduced relative to v0.5.0 baseline.
At least 80% of participants correctly identify where audio/model data is stored after relevant workflows.
At least 80% correctly distinguish Vocabulary from Voice model after completing both tasks.
No participant must discover a required action only by hovering.
High-severity usability findings are fixed or explicitly accepted through a documented human release decision.

privacySafeArtifactUpdate.update: Update docs/research/v0.6-release-usability-study.json and docs/research/v0.6-release-usability.md with aggregate results only.
allowed: participant counts by broad cohort category
explicit human release decision if accepting unresolved findings
forbidden: screen recordings or screenshots containing participant data

closeIssue255OnlyWhen: The aggregate artifact shows all #255 gates pass, or
A separate explicit human release decision accepts the unresolved gate state and is recorded in the artifact.
notSatisfiedBy: Automated axe, keyboard, visual, copy-budget, or semantic tests alone.
The #258 release publication approval alone, which permits v0.6.0 publication but does not by itself verify #255 participant usability gates.
